     The Numbers Business Report

How to be Single (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $46,843,513Details
International Box Office $65,700,000
Worldwide Box Office $112,543,513
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $3,511,968
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,726,713
Total $5,238,681
Further financial details...

Synopsis

There’s a right way to be single, a wrong way to be single, and then…there’s Alice. And Robin. Lucy. Meg. Tom. David. New York City is full of lonely hearts seeking the right match, be it a love connection, a hook-up, or something in the middle. And somewhere between the teasing texts and one-night stands, what these unmarrieds all have in common is the need to learn how to be single in a world filled with ever-evolving definitions of love. Sleeping around in the city that never sleeps was never so much fun.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$17,878,911 (38.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.62 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:41.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$37,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,343 opening theaters/3,357 max. theaters, 3.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $64,768,603

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 12th, 2016 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: February 8th, 2016 (Wide) (Taiwan)
February 10th, 2016 (Wide) (Belgium)
February 11th, 2016 (Wide) (Ukraine)
February 11th, 2016 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
February 11th, 2016 (Wide) (Portugal)
Video Release: May 3rd, 2016 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for sexual content and strong language throughout.
(Rating bulletin 2401 (Cert #50108), 11/25/2015)
Running Time: 110 minutes
Geographical setting: New York, New York City
Plot point: Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance
Subgenre: Romantic Comedy
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: New Line Cinema, Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures, Flower Films, Wrigley Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

There’s a right way to be single, a wrong way to be single, and then…there’s Alice. And Robin. Lucy. Meg. Tom. David. New York City is full of lonely hearts seeking the right match, be it a love connection, a hook-up, or something in the middle. And somewhere between the teasing texts and one-night stands, what these unmarrieds all have in common is the need to learn how to be single in a world filled with ever-evolving definitions of love. Sleeping around in the city that never sleeps was never so much fun.
